# dbx CLI Reference
# Auto-generated — do not edit manually.
# Source: go run ./cmd/docgen

## dbxcli
  dbx — multi-database management platform
  dbx is a multi-database management platform supporting Oracle and PostgreSQL.
  
  Commands use named parameters (emcli-style):
    dbxcli <domain> <action> entity_name=<name> entity_type=<type> [key=value ...]
  Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)

## dbxcli db
  Oracle database read-only operations
  Read-only Oracle database operations covering sessions, tablespaces,
  users, schemas, SQL execution, redo logs, undo, parameters, and advisors.
  
  Requires a target with an Oracle database endpoint configured.
  Flags:
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli db advisor
  Oracle advisor operations
  Query Oracle built-in advisor recommendations (DBA_ADVISOR_RECOMMENDATIONS). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db advisor segment
  Show segment advisor recommendations
  Show segment advisor recommendations for table/index shrink, compression, and space reclamation.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db advisor segment --target prod-db

## dbxcli db advisor sql-tuning
  List SQL tuning advisor tasks
  List SQL tuning advisor tasks and their recommendations (DBA_ADVISOR_TASKS).
  Examples:
    dbxcli db advisor sql-tuning --target prod-db

## dbxcli db parameter
  Oracle init parameter operations
  Query Oracle initialization parameters (V$PARAMETER, V$SYSTEM_PARAMETER). Read-only — use EE for SET/RESET.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db parameter describe
  Describe a specific parameter
  Show full details for a parameter including description, default, range, and whether it requires restart.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter describe name=sga_target --target prod-db

## dbxcli db parameter hidden
  List hidden underscore parameters
  List hidden (underscore-prefixed) parameters. Use with caution — these are unsupported by Oracle.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter hidden --target prod-db

## dbxcli db parameter list
  List all visible parameters
  List all non-hidden initialization parameters with current values and whether they are modified.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter list --target prod-db
      dbxcli db parameter list --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db parameter modified
  List non-default parameters
  List only parameters that have been changed from their default values.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db parameter modified --target prod-db

## dbxcli db redo
  Oracle redo log operations
  Query Oracle redo log groups and switch history (V$LOG, V$LOG_HISTORY). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db redo list
  List redo log groups
  List all redo log groups with status, size, member count, and sequence number.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db redo list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db redo switch-history
  Show log switch frequency
  Show redo log switch frequency per hour — useful for sizing redo logs.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db redo switch-history --target prod-db

## dbxcli db schema
  Oracle schema browser
  Browse Oracle schemas and their objects (DBA_OBJECTS, DBA_TABLES, DBA_INDEXES). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db schema describe
  Describe a specific object
  Show DDL and metadata for a specific database object (table, index, view, package, etc.).
  Examples:
    dbxcli db schema describe owner=HR object_name=EMPLOYEES --target prod-db

## dbxcli db schema list
  List schemas with object counts
  List all schemas with counts of tables, indexes, views, packages, and other objects.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db schema list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db schema objects
  List objects in a schema
  List all objects in a given schema filtered by object type.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db schema objects owner=HR --target prod-db
      dbxcli db schema objects owner=HR object_type=TABLE --target prod-db

## dbxcli db session
  Oracle session operations
  Query and inspect Oracle database sessions (V$SESSION). Read-only — no session modification.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db session describe
  Describe a session by SID
  Show detailed information for a specific session including SQL, wait event, and program.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db session describe sid=142 --target prod-db

## dbxcli db session list
  List active user sessions
  List all active user sessions from V$SESSION excluding background processes.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db session list --target prod-db
      dbxcli db session list --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db session top-waiters
  Show top sessions by wait time
  Show sessions with the highest cumulative wait time, useful for identifying performance bottlenecks.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db session top-waiters --target prod-db

## dbxcli db sql
  Read-only SQL execution
  Execute read-only SQL statements against an Oracle database.
  Only SELECT statements are permitted — DML/DDL is blocked by the ReadOnlyGuard.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db sql exec
  Execute a read-only SELECT statement
  Execute a SELECT query and return results. DML/DDL statements are rejected.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db sql exec query="SELECT sysdate FROM dual" --target prod-db
      dbxcli db sql exec query="SELECT username, account_status FROM dba_users" --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db sql explain
  Generate execution plan for a SELECT
  Generate EXPLAIN PLAN output for a SELECT statement without executing it.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db sql explain query="SELECT * FROM hr.employees WHERE department_id = 10" --target prod-db

## dbxcli db tablespace
  Oracle tablespace operations
  Query Oracle tablespace metadata, usage metrics, and datafile details (DBA_TABLESPACES, DBA_DATA_FILES). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db tablespace describe
  Describe tablespace datafiles
  Show datafiles belonging to a tablespace with sizes, autoextend status, and paths.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db tablespace describe name=USERS --target prod-db

## dbxcli db tablespace list
  List tablespaces with usage metrics
  List all tablespaces with total size, used space, free space, and percentage utilization.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db tablespace list --target prod-db
      dbxcli db tablespace list --target prod-db --format json

## dbxcli db tablespace usage
  Show aggregated tablespace usage summary
  Show aggregated storage utilization across all tablespaces with alert thresholds.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db tablespace usage --target prod-db

## dbxcli db undo
  Oracle undo/rollback operations
  Query Oracle undo tablespace usage and rollback segment details (V$UNDOSTAT, DBA_UNDO_EXTENTS). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db undo list
  List undo tablespace usage
  Show undo tablespace utilization including active, unexpired, and expired extents.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db undo list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db undo segments
  Show undo segment details
  Show individual rollback segment status, size, and transaction counts.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db undo segments --target prod-db

## dbxcli db user
  Oracle user operations
  Query Oracle database users, roles, privileges, and profiles (DBA_USERS, DBA_ROLE_PRIVS). Read-only.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli db user describe
  Describe user with roles and privileges
  Show detailed user information including granted roles, system/object privileges, and quotas.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db user describe username=SCOTT --target prod-db

## dbxcli db user list
  List database users
  List all database users with account status, default tablespace, and profile.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db user list --target prod-db

## dbxcli db user profiles
  List database profiles
  List all database profiles with their resource limits and password parameters.
  Examples:
    dbxcli db user profiles --target prod-db

## dbxcli license
  Manage dbx license
  Manage the dbx license. Licenses are Ed25519-signed JWT tokens stored at ~/.dbx/license.jwt.
  Without a license, only OSS (Community) features are available.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli license activate
  Activate a license file
  Activate a license JWT file. The file is validated, copied to ~/.dbx/license.jwt, and EE features are unlocked.
  Flags:
    --file  path to license JWT file
  Examples:
    dbxcli license activate --file /path/to/license.jwt

## dbxcli license status
  Show license status
  Show current license status including tier, entity count, expiry date, and grace period.
  Examples:
    dbxcli license status

## dbxcli linux
  Oracle Linux host management over SSH
  Oracle Linux system management operations executed over SSH.
  Covers package management, kernel parameters, storage/LVM, network, and security.
  
  Requires a target with SSH endpoint configured (oracle_host entity_type).
  Flags:
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli linux kernel
  Kernel parameter management
  Kernel parameter management via sysctl and /proc filesystem over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux kernel hugepages
  Set hugepages count (confirm-gated)

## dbxcli linux kernel info
  Show OS/kernel info (uname)

## dbxcli linux kernel param-list
  List all sysctl parameters

## dbxcli linux kernel param-set
  Set a sysctl parameter (confirm-gated)

## dbxcli linux network
  Network diagnostics
  Network diagnostics via ip, nmcli, chronyc, and ss over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux network bond-status
  Show network bond status

## dbxcli linux network dns-check
  Check DNS resolver configuration

## dbxcli linux network nic-list
  List NICs with addresses

## dbxcli linux network ntp-status
  Check NTP synchronization

## dbxcli linux package
  RPM/DNF package management
  RPM package queries and DNF install/update operations via SSH (rpm, dnf commands).
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux package info
  Show package details
  Show detailed information for a specific RPM package.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package info name=oracle-database-ee-19c --target db01

## dbxcli linux package install
  Install a package via DNF (confirm-gated)
  Install a package using dnf. Requires confirmation before execution.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package install name=oracle-database-preinstall-19c --target db01

## dbxcli linux package list
  List installed RPM packages
  List all installed RPM packages with version and architecture.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package list --target web01

## dbxcli linux package update
  Update a package via DNF (confirm-gated)
  Update a package using dnf. Requires confirmation before execution.
  Examples:
    dbxcli linux package update name=oracle-database-preinstall-19c --target db01

## dbxcli linux security
  Security status checks
  Security status checks: SELinux, firewall rules, and running services over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux security firewall-list
  List firewall rules

## dbxcli linux security selinux-status
  Check SELinux status

## dbxcli linux security service-status
  List running services

## dbxcli linux storage
  Storage and LVM management
  Linux storage and LVM management via pvs/vgs/lvs, lvcreate, df, lsblk over SSH.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli linux storage disk-usage
  Show disk usage

## dbxcli linux storage lv-create
  Create a logical volume (confirm-gated)

## dbxcli linux storage lv-list
  List logical volumes

## dbxcli linux storage pv-list
  List physical volumes

## dbxcli linux storage vg-list
  List volume groups

## dbxcli mcp
  Start MCP server
  Start the dbx MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for AI integration.
  Supports stdio transport (default) and SSE transport for remote connections.
  Flags:
    --port  SSE transport port (0 = stdio) (default: 0)
  Examples:
    dbxcli mcp                  # stdio transport (for Claude Code, IDE extensions)
      dbxcli mcp --port 3001      # SSE transport (for remote MCP clients)

## dbxcli pg
  PostgreSQL database operations
  PostgreSQL database operations covering connections, queries, schema browsing,
  CRUD, DBA, performance, security, replication, high availability, backup,
  migration, observability, multi-tenancy, WAL, CNPG, disaster recovery,
  RAG/pgvector, Vault credentials, and policy checks.
  
  Requires a target with a PostgreSQL database endpoint configured.
  Flags:
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli pg audit
  Audit logging
  PostgreSQL audit logging — pgAudit status, log analysis, and DDL history.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg audit ddl-history
  Show DDL change history
  Show the history of DDL changes (CREATE, ALTER, DROP) from event triggers or logs.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg audit ddl-history --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg audit log-analysis
  Analyze PostgreSQL logs
  Analyze PostgreSQL server logs for errors, warnings, and patterns.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg audit log-analysis --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg audit pgaudit-status
  Show pgAudit configuration
  Show the current pgAudit extension configuration and logging settings.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg audit pgaudit-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg backup
  Backup operations
  PostgreSQL backup operations — pgBackRest and Barman management.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg backup barman-status
  Show Barman backup status
  Show the current Barman backup status and catalog.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg backup barman-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg backup pgbackrest-backup
  Trigger pgBackRest backup
  Trigger a pgBackRest backup of the specified type and stanza.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg backup pgbackrest-backup type=full stanza=main --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg backup pgbackrest-restore
  Restore from pgBackRest
  Restore from a pgBackRest backup with optional point-in-time target.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg backup pgbackrest-restore stanza=main target_time="2026-01-01 12:00:00" --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg backup pgbackrest-status
  Show pgBackRest backup status
  Show the current pgBackRest backup status and history.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg backup pgbackrest-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg cnpg
  CloudNativePG
  CloudNativePG cluster management — list, status, promote, restart, backup, and switchover.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg cnpg backup-status
  Show CNPG backup status
  Show backup status for a CloudNativePG cluster.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg cnpg backup-status name=my-cluster namespace=default --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg cnpg cluster-status
  Show CNPG cluster status
  Show detailed status for a specific CloudNativePG cluster.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg cnpg cluster-status name=my-cluster namespace=default --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg cnpg clusters
  List CNPG clusters
  List all CloudNativePG clusters in the specified namespace.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg cnpg clusters namespace=default --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg cnpg promote
  Promote CNPG replica
  Promote a CloudNativePG replica to primary.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg cnpg promote name=my-cluster namespace=default --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg cnpg restart
  Rolling restart CNPG cluster
  Perform a rolling restart of a CloudNativePG cluster.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg cnpg restart name=my-cluster namespace=default --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg cnpg switchover
  Trigger CNPG switchover
  Trigger a switchover on a CloudNativePG cluster.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg cnpg switchover name=my-cluster namespace=default --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg comply
  Compliance checks
  PostgreSQL compliance checks — CIS benchmarks, password policies, encryption, and privilege audits.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg comply cis-benchmark
  Run CIS benchmark checks
  Run CIS PostgreSQL benchmark checks against the database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg comply cis-benchmark --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg comply encryption-status
  Check encryption at rest
  Check the status of data encryption at rest.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg comply encryption-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg comply password-policy
  Check password policies
  Check password policy enforcement on all database roles.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg comply password-policy --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg comply privilege-audit
  Audit excessive privileges
  Audit roles for excessive privileges (superuser, CREATEROLE, etc.).
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg comply privilege-audit --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg comply report
  Generate compliance report
  Generate a comprehensive compliance report covering all checks.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg comply report --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg connect
  PostgreSQL connection management
  Manage PostgreSQL connection profiles — add, remove, switch, and inspect connections.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg connect add
  Register a new connection profile
  Register a new PostgreSQL connection profile with host, port, database, and user.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg connect add name=prod host=db01.example.com port=5432 database=app user=admin --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg connect list
  List all connection profiles
  List all registered PostgreSQL connection profiles.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg connect list --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg connect remove
  Remove a connection profile
  Remove an existing PostgreSQL connection profile by name.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg connect remove name=prod --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg connect status
  Show current connection status
  Show the current active PostgreSQL connection status and details.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg connect status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg connect switch
  Switch active connection
  Switch the active PostgreSQL connection to a different profile.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg connect switch name=staging --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg crud
  Data manipulation
  Insert, update, delete, and upsert rows in PostgreSQL tables.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg crud delete
  Delete rows
  Delete rows matching the WHERE clause from the specified table.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg crud delete schema=public table=users where="id=1" --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg crud insert
  Insert a row
  Insert a new row into the specified table.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg crud insert schema=public table=users data='{"name":"alice"}' --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg crud update
  Update rows
  Update rows matching the WHERE clause in the specified table.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg crud update schema=public table=users set='{"active":true}' where="id=1" --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg crud upsert
  Upsert a row
  Insert a row or update it on conflict using the specified conflict key.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg crud upsert schema=public table=users data='{"id":1,"name":"alice"}' conflict=id --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba
  DBA operations
  PostgreSQL DBA operations — server settings, connections, locks, long queries, and management.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg dba blocker-tree
  Show blocking lock tree
  Show the blocking lock tree — which sessions block which.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba blocker-tree --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba cancel-query
  Cancel a running query
  Cancel a running query by backend PID (pg_cancel_backend).
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba cancel-query pid=12345 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba connections
  Show active connections
  Show all active connections from pg_stat_activity.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba connections --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba database-sizes
  Show database sizes
  Show the size of each database on the server.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba database-sizes --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba locks
  Show lock information
  Show current lock information from pg_locks.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba locks --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba long-queries
  Show long-running queries
  Show queries that have been running longer than the configured threshold.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba long-queries --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba reload
  Reload configuration
  Reload PostgreSQL server configuration without restart (pg_reload_conf).
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba reload --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba settings
  Show server settings
  Show current PostgreSQL server configuration settings.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba settings --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba terminate
  Terminate a backend
  Terminate a backend process by PID (pg_terminate_backend).
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba terminate pid=12345 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba uptime
  Show server uptime
  Show how long the PostgreSQL server has been running.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba uptime --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba version
  Show PostgreSQL version
  Show the PostgreSQL server version string.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba version --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba-adv
  Advanced DBA operations
  Advanced PostgreSQL DBA operations — vacuum status, bloat estimation, cache analysis, and unused indexes.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg dba-adv cache-hit
  Show buffer cache hit ratio
  Show the buffer cache hit ratio from pg_stat_database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba-adv cache-hit --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba-adv index-bloat
  Estimate index bloat
  Estimate index bloat across the database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba-adv index-bloat --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba-adv table-bloat
  Estimate table bloat
  Estimate table bloat across the database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba-adv table-bloat --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba-adv unused-indexes
  List unused indexes
  List indexes with zero or very low scan counts.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba-adv unused-indexes --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dba-adv vacuum-status
  Show vacuum status
  Show autovacuum and manual vacuum status for all tables.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dba-adv vacuum-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr
  Disaster recovery
  PostgreSQL disaster recovery — configuration, status, switchover, failover,
  WAL shipping, MinIO archive, PITR, runbooks, and compliance reporting.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg dr config-delete
  Delete DR configuration
  Delete a disaster recovery configuration by name.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr config-delete name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr config-get
  Get DR configuration
  Get a specific disaster recovery configuration by name.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr config-get name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr config-list
  List DR configurations
  List all disaster recovery configurations.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr config-list --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr config-set
  Set DR configuration
  Create or update a disaster recovery configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr config-set name=prod-dr primary=dc1-pg standby=dc2-pg --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr failover
  Execute DR failover
  Execute an emergency DR failover to the standby.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr failover name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr minio-status
  Show MinIO WAL archive status
  Show the MinIO WAL archive status and bucket health.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr minio-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr minio-verify
  Verify MinIO archive integrity
  Verify the integrity of the MinIO WAL archive.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr minio-verify --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr monitor
  Start continuous DR monitoring
  Start continuous disaster recovery monitoring for a configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr monitor name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr pitr-restore
  Point-in-time recovery
  Perform a point-in-time recovery to a specific timestamp.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr pitr-restore name=prod-dr target_time="2026-01-01 12:00:00" --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr pitr-timeline
  Show PITR timeline
  Show the PITR timeline and available recovery points.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr pitr-timeline name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr report
  Generate DR compliance report
  Generate a disaster recovery compliance report.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr report --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr runbook
  Generate DR runbook
  Generate a disaster recovery runbook for a configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr runbook name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr status
  Show DR status
  Show the current disaster recovery status for a configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr status name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr switchover
  Execute DR switchover
  Execute a controlled DR switchover from primary to standby.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr switchover name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr sync-status
  Show replication sync status
  Show the replication synchronization status for a DR configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr sync-status name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr test-failover
  Dry-run failover test
  Run a dry-run failover test without actually switching over.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr test-failover name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr validate
  Validate DR setup
  Validate that the DR setup is correctly configured and operational.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr validate name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg dr wal-shipping
  Configure WAL shipping
  Configure WAL shipping for a DR configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg dr wal-shipping name=prod-dr --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha
  High availability
  PostgreSQL high availability — Patroni cluster management, PgBouncer pools, and VIP failover.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg ha patroni-config
  Show Patroni configuration
  Show the current Patroni DCS configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha patroni-config --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha patroni-failover
  Initiate Patroni failover
  Initiate a Patroni failover to the specified target node.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha patroni-failover target_node=replica1 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha patroni-restart
  Restart Patroni member
  Restart a specific Patroni cluster member.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha patroni-restart member=node1 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha patroni-status
  Show Patroni cluster status
  Show the current Patroni cluster status including leader, replicas, and timeline.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha patroni-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha patroni-switchover
  Initiate Patroni switchover
  Initiate a controlled Patroni switchover to the specified target node.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha patroni-switchover target_node=replica1 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-pause
  Pause PgBouncer connections
  Pause all PgBouncer connection pools.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-pause --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-pools
  List PgBouncer pools
  List all PgBouncer connection pools with their configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-pools --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-resume
  Resume PgBouncer connections
  Resume all PgBouncer connection pools.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-resume --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-status
  Show PgBouncer pool status
  Show PgBouncer connection pool status and statistics.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha pgbouncer-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg ha vip-status
  Show VIP failover status
  Show the virtual IP failover status.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg ha vip-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg health
  Cluster health
  Run comprehensive PostgreSQL cluster health checks.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg health check
  Run comprehensive health check
  Run a comprehensive health check covering connections, replication, locks, and storage.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg health check --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg migrate
  Migration operations
  PostgreSQL migration operations — pg_upgrade, logical migration, and foreign data wrappers.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg migrate fdw-setup
  Setup foreign data wrapper
  Setup a foreign data wrapper to access a remote server and schema.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg migrate fdw-setup server=remote-db schema=public --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg migrate logical-migrate
  Logical migration via pub/sub
  Perform a logical migration using publication/subscription between source and target.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg migrate logical-migrate source=old-db target=new-db --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg migrate pg-upgrade-check
  Pre-check pg_upgrade compatibility
  Run pg_upgrade compatibility checks without performing the upgrade.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg migrate pg-upgrade-check --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg migrate pg-upgrade-run
  Run pg_upgrade
  Run pg_upgrade from the old version to the new version.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg migrate pg-upgrade-run old_version=15 new_version=16 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg observe
  Observability
  PostgreSQL observability — Prometheus metrics, slow queries, activity, and background writer stats.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg observe metrics
  Export Prometheus-compatible metrics
  Export PostgreSQL metrics in Prometheus-compatible format.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg observe metrics --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg observe slow-log
  Analyze slow query log
  Analyze the PostgreSQL slow query log for patterns.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg observe slow-log --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg observe stat-activity
  Show pg_stat_activity snapshot
  Show a snapshot of pg_stat_activity with all backend details.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg observe stat-activity --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg observe stat-bgwriter
  Show background writer stats
  Show background writer statistics from pg_stat_bgwriter.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg observe stat-bgwriter --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg perf
  Performance analysis
  PostgreSQL performance analysis — pg_stat_statements and wait events.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg perf stat-statements
  Show pg_stat_statements top queries
  Show top queries from pg_stat_statements ordered by total time.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg perf stat-statements --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg perf wait-events
  Show wait event statistics
  Show wait event statistics from pg_stat_activity.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg perf wait-events --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg policy
  Policy engine
  PostgreSQL policy engine — run policy checks and list available policies.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg policy check
  Run policy check against database
  Run a specific policy check against the target database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg policy check policy_name=no-superuser-apps --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg policy list
  List available policies
  List all available policies that can be checked against databases.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg policy list --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg query
  SQL query execution
  Execute SQL queries against a PostgreSQL database — SELECT, EXPLAIN, and prepared statements.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg query exec
  Execute a read-only SELECT
  Execute a read-only SELECT query and return the results.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg query exec query="SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10" --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg query explain
  Generate execution plan
  Generate an EXPLAIN ANALYZE execution plan for a query.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg query explain query="SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status='pending'" --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg query prepared
  Execute a prepared statement
  Execute a prepared statement by name with the given parameters.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg query prepared name=get_user params="[1]" --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rag
  RAG/pgvector
  PostgreSQL RAG and pgvector operations — vector collections, similarity search, ingestion, and configuration.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg rag collection-create
  Create vector collection
  Create a new vector collection with the specified dimensions.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rag collection-create name=embeddings dimensions=1536 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rag collection-drop
  Drop vector collection
  Drop a vector collection and all its data.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rag collection-drop name=embeddings --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rag collections
  List vector collections
  List all vector collections (tables with vector columns).
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rag collections --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rag config
  Show pgvector configuration
  Show the current pgvector extension configuration.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rag config --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rag index-status
  Show vector index stats
  Show vector index statistics for a collection.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rag index-status collection=embeddings --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rag ingest
  Ingest documents
  Ingest documents into a vector collection from the specified source.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rag ingest collection=embeddings source=/path/to/docs --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rag search
  Similarity search
  Perform a similarity search against a vector collection.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rag search collection=embeddings query="machine learning" limit=10 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rbac
  Role-based access control
  PostgreSQL role-based access control — list, grant, revoke, and inspect effective permissions.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg rbac effective-perms
  Show effective permissions
  Show effective permissions for a role including inherited grants.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rbac effective-perms role=app_user --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rbac grant
  Grant role or privilege
  Grant a role or privilege to a target role.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rbac grant role=readonly target=app_user --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rbac list-roles
  List all roles with members
  List all roles with their member relationships.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rbac list-roles --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg rbac revoke
  Revoke role or privilege
  Revoke a role or privilege from a target role.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg rbac revoke role=readonly target=app_user --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg repl
  Replication management
  PostgreSQL replication management — status, slots, lag, and logical subscriptions.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg repl lag
  Show replication lag
  Show replication lag for all standbys.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg repl lag --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg repl slots
  List replication slots
  List all replication slots with their status and lag.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg repl slots --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg repl status
  Show replication status
  Show the current replication status from pg_stat_replication.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg repl status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg repl subscriptions
  List logical subscriptions
  List all logical replication subscriptions.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg repl subscriptions --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema
  Schema browser
  Browse PostgreSQL schemas, tables, views, functions, types, sequences, indexes, and extensions.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg schema extensions
  List installed extensions
  List all installed PostgreSQL extensions.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema extensions --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema functions
  List functions
  List all functions in the specified schema.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema functions schema=public --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema indexes
  List indexes
  List all indexes for a table in the specified schema.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema indexes schema=public table=users --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema schemas
  List schemas
  List all schemas in the database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema schemas --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema sequences
  List sequences
  List all sequences in the specified schema.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema sequences schema=public --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema table-describe
  Describe a table
  Show column details, constraints, and metadata for a specific table.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema table-describe schema=public table=users --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema tables
  List tables
  List all tables in the specified schema.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema tables schema=public --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema types
  List custom types
  List all custom types in the specified schema.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema types schema=public --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg schema views
  List views
  List all views in the specified schema.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg schema views schema=public --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg security
  Security audit
  PostgreSQL security audit — roles, row-level security, SSL, and pg_hba.conf.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg security pg-hba
  Parse pg_hba.conf rules
  Parse and display the active pg_hba.conf authentication rules.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg security pg-hba --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg security roles
  List roles and privileges
  List all PostgreSQL roles with their privileges and membership.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg security roles --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg security row-security
  Show row-level security policies
  Show all row-level security (RLS) policies defined on tables.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg security row-security --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg security ssl-status
  Show SSL/TLS connection status
  Show SSL/TLS connection status for all active connections.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg security ssl-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg tenant
  Multi-tenant management
  PostgreSQL multi-tenant management — provision, deprovision, list, migrate, and check tenant databases.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg tenant deprovision
  Remove tenant database
  Remove a tenant database and all associated resources.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg tenant deprovision tenant_id=acme-corp --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg tenant list
  List tenant databases
  List all tenant databases with their status and metadata.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg tenant list --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg tenant migrate
  Run tenant schema migration
  Run schema migrations on a specific tenant database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg tenant migrate tenant_id=acme-corp --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg tenant provision
  Provision new tenant database
  Provision a new tenant database with schema and role setup.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg tenant provision tenant_id=acme-corp --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg tenant status
  Show tenant health status
  Show health status for a specific tenant database.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg tenant status tenant_id=acme-corp --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg vault
  Vault credential management
  HashiCorp Vault integration for PostgreSQL credential rotation, leases, and revocation.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg vault revoke
  Revoke database credentials
  Revoke a Vault database credential lease by ID.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg vault revoke lease_id=database/creds/app-readonly/abc123 --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg vault rotate
  Rotate database credentials
  Rotate database credentials for the specified Vault role.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg vault rotate role=app-readonly --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg vault status
  Show Vault lease status
  Show the current Vault lease status for database credentials.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg vault status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg wal
  WAL management
  PostgreSQL WAL (Write-Ahead Log) management — status, archiving, size, retention, and replay lag.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml
    --target  target name (from ~/.dbx/targets/)

## dbxcli pg wal archive-status
  Check WAL archiving status
  Check the WAL archiving status and any failures.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg wal archive-status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg wal replay-lag
  Show WAL replay lag on standbys
  Show WAL replay lag on all standby servers.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg wal replay-lag --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg wal retention
  Show WAL retention policy
  Show the configured WAL retention policy and current usage.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg wal retention --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg wal size
  Show WAL directory size
  Show the current WAL directory size on disk.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg wal size --target prod-pg

## dbxcli pg wal status
  Show WAL generation stats
  Show WAL generation statistics and current LSN.
  Examples:
    dbxcli pg wal status --target prod-pg

## dbxcli serve
  Start REST API server
  Start the dbx REST API server. Routes are auto-generated from the Cobra command tree.
  The API exposes all CLI operations as HTTP endpoints with JWT authentication.
  Flags:
    --auth-mode  auth mode: jwt, basic, none (default: jwt)
    --port  listen port (default: 8080)
  Examples:
    dbxcli serve
      dbxcli serve --port 9090 --auth-mode basic

## dbxcli target
  Manage system targets
  Manage the target registry — databases, hosts, and services that dbx connects to.
  Targets are stored in ~/.dbx/targets/ as YAML files.
  Inherited Flags:
    --format  output format: table, json, yaml

## dbxcli target add
  Register a new target
  Register a new target (database, host, or service) in the target registry.
  Examples:
    dbxcli target add entity_name=prod-db entity_type=oracle_db host=db01.example.com port=1521 service=ORCL
      dbxcli target add entity_name=web01 entity_type=oracle_host host=web01.example.com

## dbxcli target list
  List registered targets
  List all registered targets with their type, host, and connection status.
  Examples:
    dbxcli target list
      dbxcli target list entity_type=oracle_db

## dbxcli target test
  Test connectivity to a target
  Test network and authentication connectivity to a registered target.
  Examples:
    dbxcli target test entity_name=prod-db
